entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 21 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-21 07:36AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Elevated flooding risk lingers over NSW for months to come
  • Perrottet calls for Sidoti to resign his seat after ICAC corruption finding
  • Latex, frozen sperm and poo-eating beetles: The MCA gets its weird on
  • Court hears of police failures in singer Jaguar Jonze’s sexual assault case
  • Five years’ jail for driver who hit two police officers at Maccas then sped off
  • ‘We have the right coach’: Hunt says Griffin’s future key to new Dragons deal
  • Coercive control bill can help the struggle against domestic violence
  • The NRL has fathers coaching sons, but what about a brother coaching his sibling?
  • Race-by-race preview and tips for Tamworth on Friday
  • Australia news LIVE: Livestock industry under threat as foot and mouth disease detected in imported meat; COVID cases continue to grow across the nation
  • Pay rises, not cupcakes: Childcare educators to strike nationally
  • Albanese ‘astonished’ by Morrison’s anti-government comments
  • ‘I’m pumped’: New senators assemble for an upper house boot camp
  • Indonesia pledges to halt foot and mouth outbreak as virus fragments found in Australia
  • Best of cartoons, July 21, 2022
  • Cartoons of the week
  • Albanese goes back to the future for governing
  • Business lobby boss wants wages in line with living costs in jobs summit agenda
  • Climate change top of the agenda as Labor kick-starts new parliament with four bills
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • Best of cartoons, July 20, 2022
  • RBA faces reform not revolution but the board set for a shake-up
  • Waste company fined more than $500,000 after worker killed by reversing skip loader
  • Perrottet concedes disappointment over trade trip being overshadowed by Barilaro controversy
  • ‘Guinea pig’ surfers test foot and mouth quarantine response
  • Michael Slater charged with breach of bail after police welfare check
  • Police release CCTV after 16-year-old stabbed in Sydney’s west during ‘fight over girl’
  • Unfair to blame Labor for neglect of environment
  • In the Herald: July 21, 1955
  • A rat’s tale
  • Six arrested after police cars allegedly rammed by truck carrying cocaine worth $50 million
  • Qantas pilots declare ‘mayday’ due to low fuel on transcontinental flight
  • US estimates 60,000 Russian casualties from Ukraine invasion
  • Scientists uncover the origin of mammal milestone: warm-bloodedness
  • Letter reveals China pressuring UN rights chief to bury Xinjiang report
  • Rishi is dishy but Truss is the true-blue choice for British PM
  • Taliban force Australian journalist to apologise for child sex slave reports
  • Donald Trump called officials ‘this week’ seeking overturn of 2020 election
  • COVID outbreak hits last ‘bigger nation’, two weeks before border due to open
  • Quidditch becomes quadball as sport distances itself from author JK Rowling
  • Rishi Sunak or Liz Truss to become Britain’s next prime minister
  • From the Archives, 1942: Remembering the war on the Kokoda Track
  • China tightens negotiations with Australia over iron ore
  • Sri Lanka’s Wickremesinghe picked for new president, despite protests
  • Premier implores Queenslanders to wear a mask as new cases surpass 11,500
  • Second air-taxi company enters SEQ market targeting Olympic sky rush
  • Former TV reporter Trent Evans jailed over car crash that killed friend
  • Woodside floats gas imports as ‘quick fix’ for Victorian energy crisis
  • Sydney to get a new rooftop bar as InterContinental hotel gets a $110m facelift
  • Aussie biotech’s prostate cancer imaging drug booms in the US
  • Are blockchain video games a fad, or the future?
  • The Wrap: Tech stocks win tug-of-war to push ASX higher
  • ‘Brings considerable value’: Josh Frydenberg lands job at Goldman Sachs
  • Australia (and the world) is still grappling with travel chaos. But it could have been worse
  • Zip Co shares surge as it vows to slow cash burn
  • Environment minister requests work pause on $4.5b Pilbara urea plant
  • TPG Telecom slams Berejiklian claims on network-sharing deal
  • Breaking bad: How BlackRock lost $1.7 trillion dollars in six months
  • Herald and Age journalists recognised by Kennedy Award nominations
  • ‘Son of Concorde’ jet moves a step closer to reality
  • Climate policy will take pressure off budget, Treasury secretary says
  • Shell to lock out striking workers from world’s biggest ship from Monday
  • New era: Amazon CEO breaks from the ‘Bezos way’
  • Rio Tinto pays nearly $1 billion in tax avoidance settlement
  • Domestic bliss becomes a living nightmare in this twisty thriller
  • ‘Nothing about us without us’: The idea that sparked Melbourne’s newest festival
  • The strange and compelling play that has no characters
  • The women of Hairspray on racism, sexism and having to hustle
  • The blood, tears and beheadings behind the ‘sickest band in the world’
  • Game of Thrones prequel fires up Comic-Con with first full trailer
  • Billy Porter’s romcom starts like a bad Clueless remake. How wrong I was
  • Like chardonnay: How the ‘new’ Opera House sounds
  • How to be a nepotism baby (it’s not as hard as the Hadids make out)
  • We’re not in Kandos anymore! How art transformed an out of luck town
  • Art theft and forgery fuse in a grungy tour of ’90s Australia
  • Not the same as it was: One Direction were always great. It’s me who sucked.
  • Boris Johnson’s demise proves a boon for the puzzle pages
  • Crazy, stupid, love on show in intimate and edgy exhibition
  • Overwhelmed by environmental disaster? Here’s a scorecard to inspire optimism
  • The case for a global fossil fuel non-proliferation treaty - from an island nation in peril
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Watching Japan reckon with a rare shooting, through an American prism
  • Russia’s Gazprom resumes gas flows to Germany after Nord Stream maintenance
  • Myanmar’s junta can’t win the civil war it started
  • Lourdes Grobet, photographer of lucha libre’s masked stars, dies at 81
  • Ukraine live briefing: Russia expands territorial ambitions as U.S. weighs sending fighter jets to Kyiv
  • Why European homes (usually) don’t have air conditioning
  • U.S., allies weigh providing Ukraine fighter jets to counter Russia
  • Italy’s Draghi falls short as major parties sit out confidence vote
  • Turkish strike kills at least 8 Iraqi tourists in northern resort
  • It’s Rishi Sunak vs. Liz Truss for U.K. leader; Boris Johnson says ‘Hasta la vista, baby!’
  • How the next U.K. prime minister will be chosen
  • Ukraine’s first lady asks Congress for more arms to counter Russia
  • E.U. urges gas rationing ahead of ‘likely’ cutoff by Russia
  • Brutal heat dome moves east, with Central Europe sweltering
  • Amid summer heat wave, Germany worries about having enough gas for winter
  • Sri Lanka picks establishment figure as president, risking more unrest
  • Quidditch is now quadball, distancing game from J.K. Rowling, league says
  • Russian tennis star criticizes Ukraine war in rare dissent, comes out as gay
  • Ukraine’s farmers become the latest target of Russian missiles
  • Methanol found in bodies of 21 teens who died in South African nightclub
  • Trees dedicated to memory of Buchenwald victims chopped down
  • World's oldest male giant panda dies at age 35 in Hong Kong
  • Syrian refugees anxious over Lebanon's plans to deport them
  • Mexican journalist complains to president of threats
  • UN official: Decades of development progress now reversed
  • Lightning strike kills one soldier, injures nine at Georgia base
  • Texas congressman faces blowback over blogger’s ‘Miss Frijoles’ attacks
  • Parents of Oxford school shooter seek to have case tossed
  • Woman says she carried dead fetus for 2 weeks after Texas abortion ban
  • Judges ordered their release from jail. They weren’t let out, lawsuit says.
  • She flew to Australia with a Subway sandwich. She was fined $1,844.
  • U.S. offers $5K to find wrongly freed suspected dognapper of Lady Gaga’s Frenchies
  • She just got accepted to medical school. She’s 13.
  • A Missouri man is suing Bass Pro over ‘lifetime guarantee’ for socks
  • Brett Kavanaugh’s neighbors: For abortion rights but tired of protests
  • Elaine Luria prepares to lead Jan. 6 hearing, connect Trump to violence
  • Even a day after Jan. 6, Trump balked at condemning the violence
  • Prosecution rests contempt case against Bannon after two witnesses
  • Rudy Giuliani ordered to testify before Georgia special grand jury
  • Prosecutors wrap up case against Bannon after two witnesses
  • Officials reorganize HHS to boost pandemic response
  • Post Politics Now: Biden calls climate crisis a ‘clear and present danger;’ House panel considers assault weapons ban
  • Trump called ‘within the last week’ to overturn Wis. election results, speaker says
  • Trump’s choices escalated tensions and set U.S. on path to Jan. 6, panel finds
  • Stablecoins would get federal rules under emerging House deal
  • Housing market slows further in June as sales, mortgage demand tumble
  • USPS will make 40% of its new trucks electric, up from 10%
  • Chipotle closes Maine store that sought to unionize
  • Amazon workers launch campaign to unionize in Albany
  • 6 key things to know about inflation-indexed bonds paying 9.62 percent
  • Popular block game ‘Minecraft’ says no to NFTs and the blockchain
  • Tesla dumps Bitcoin amid pressure from factory shutdown
  • A mid-century modern neighborhood ‘ensconced in nature’
  • New scoring system for condo and homeowner associations
  • Macron and Scholz Need a Grand Bargain on Energy
  • Sweating While Worrying About Shivering
  • Britain Is Burning Up. India Has Some Tips.
  • The ECB Has More at Play — and Risk — Than the Fed
  • How Thailand Is Blazing Trails on Gay Rights, Legalizing Marijuana
  • Understanding Monkeypox and How Outbreaks Spread
  • How Sri Lanka Landed in a Crisis and What It Means: QuickTake
  • Understanding the Unraveling of Tunisia’s Revolution
  • A bill to prevent Trump’s attempted coup is finally ready — and must pass
  • Aug. 2 offers GOP a chance to reject malignant strains of Trumpism
  • Vote for Liz Cheney. (I never thought I’d say that.)
  • Real stuff matters, because real stuff happens
  • Okay, but Cassandra cannot possibly be right *again*
  • The top Marine faces unprecedented opposition. He says that’s ‘positive.’
  • Biden has abdicated U.S. leadership on Syria to Russia and Iran
  • Some good news: Trump seems closer than ever to prosecution
  • The new proposal to prevent a future coup is surprisingly good
  • How jailed Hong Kong protesters are subjected to ‘thought work’
  • What must still be faced in the Emmett Till tragedy
  • Cartoon by Rob Rogers
  • Youngkin needs to decide where his priorities lie
  • What would Virginia have looked like if the South had won?
  • Driving is a privilege, not a right
  • Manchin’s interests run deep
  • The ugliness of our border arrives in D.C.
  • Global vaccination rates are important, too
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.content:

entry.content:

entry.title: Guardian: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • UK public finances worsen amid record debt interest costs, as ECB set to hike rates – business live
  • They’re calling it a ‘hot strike summer’ in Britain. But are the unions up to the task? | Polly Smythe
  • Chris Kirkland: ‘I didn’t know who I was, couldn’t remember where home was’
  • I am a taxi service for my teens – and I love it
  • Suspected Fabergé egg found on Russian oligarch’s superyacht, US investigators say
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Moscow restarts gas supply to Europe via Nord Stream pipeline at reduced capacity – live
  • Minecraft developers won’t allow NFTs on gaming platform
  • ‘You are not being heard’: the devastating black maternal mortality crisis in the US
  • Climate emergency is a legacy of colonialism, says Greenpeace UK
  • January 6 panel to show Trump violated law by refusing to stop Capitol attack
  • ‘Emotion and pain’ as Dutch farmers fight back against huge cuts to livestock
  • Song of the summer 2022: our writers pick their favourite tracks
  • Mysterious pink glow in sky over Australian town revealed to be from local cannabis facility
  • ‘The deepest silences’: what lies behind the Indigenous suicide crisis
  • ‘I walked into the comedy desert and a part of me died’ – the standup who became a therapist
  • ‘Vote me, get Thatcher’: why the Tories are still obsessed with the Iron Lady
  • Interest rates: three central banks facing one agonising decision | Larry Elliott
  • How a great English city sold itself to Abu Dhabi’s elite – and not even for a good price | Aditya Chakrabortty
  • Covid vaccine figures lay bare global inequality as global target missed
  • Sri Lanka braced for more unrest as new president vows crackdown on ‘fascist’ protests
  • Caster Semenya out of world 5000m as Coe signals tougher female sport rules
  • A third of Wellington’s kākā parrot chicks found to have lead in their blood
  • Ukraine war forcing China to rethink ‘how and when’ it may invade Taiwan, CIA chief says
  • When the world overwhelms, joyous rituals like weddings endure – now that’s what J-Lo and I have in common | Van Badham
  • Egypt relaxes street photography ban for tourists, up to a point
  • Russia-Ukraine war at a glance: what we know on day 148 of the invasion
  • James Harden reaches two-year, $68.8m deal to return to Philadelphia 76ers
  • Coup, Covid, climate: the triple threat chasing citizens from Myanmar’s rice bowl
  • ‘A crazy day’: Wiegman hails England for recovering from ‘setback’ to beat Spain
  • Georgia Stanway’s vision puts England on the path to salvation | Jonathan Liew
  • Unvaccinated review – the most infuriating TV show of the year so far
  • US launches environmental study for Thirty Meter telescope on Mauna Kea
  • Tesla sells 75% of its bitcoin as profits slump due to production challenges
  • Biden unveils extreme heat plan – but doesn’t declare climate emergency
  • Ivana Trump funeral: Donald Trump and children attend ‘wonderful send-off’
  • Judge acted unlawfully over hearing on Prince Philip’s will, court told
  • ‘Recipe for success’ sets up Irene Fuhrmann’s Austria for Germany tie
  • Italy’s Mario Draghi expected to resign as prime minister
  • Tories brace for ‘blue on blue dogfight’ in last round of leadership race
  • ‘I disagree’: Henrik Stenson stripped of Ryder Cup captaincy due to LIV deal
  • Police chief on Paris final ‘independent review’ is Uefa security officer
  • NSW government suggested children as young as 10 could work off $1,000 Covid fines
  • Arizona Republican censured by party over testimony on resisting Trump
  • Ukraine’s first lady pleads with US for more weapons: ‘Help us stop this terror’
  • Who will be Britain’s next prime minister, Rishi Sunak or Liz Truss? | The panel
  • ‘We missed that’: Bruno Fernandes backs strict Ten Hag’s fines for lateness
  • Nope review – Jordan Peele’s followup to Get Out and Us is a bit meh
  • AC Milan player Tiémoué Bakayoko speaks out after police held him at gunpoint
  • Stephen Colbert on extreme heatwaves: ‘Unfortunately, hell came to us’
  • Tour de France: Pogacar wins stage 17 as Vingegaard holds on and Thomas fades
  • Quidditch changes name to quadball after JK Rowling’s trans statements
  • ‘Kafka would blush’: artist Peter Max caught in legal guardianship lawsuit
  • Edwardian morals, Thatcher and bad design – why Britain's homes are so hot | Phineas Harper
  • Beware of the cobra: why snakebites are soaring in the UK
  • Magnus Carlsen will not defend his World Chess Championship title in 2023
  • Moving The Goalposts | ‘Great togetherness’ – vibrant fans amplify the joy at Euro 2022
  • How to make sweet or savoury scones – recipe | Felicity Cloake's Masterclass
  • TechScape: suspicious of TikTok? You’re not alone
  • ‘We don’t want money going to private landlords’: UK music venues turn to community ownership
  • Postcards from a 40C future: what extreme heat means for the UK
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: Financial Times: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • HSBC installs Communist party committee in Chinese investment bank
  • Didi fined over $1bn by Beijing for ‘vile’ breaches of data laws
  • Chinese city Zhengzhou sets up bailout fund as mortgage boycott spreads

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 21 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-21 07:04AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong heatwave: residents in city’s subdivided flats, rooftop huts bear brunt of scorching weather
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong health minister to meet pandemic advisers on Covid strategy amid caseload surge, ‘centralise’ communication with public
  • Goodbye, An An: Hong Kong’s beloved giant panda euthanised at Ocean Park after period of declining health from old age
  • End of story or new chapter? How Hong Kong’s independent booksellers are adapting to life under national security law
  • Sun Hung Kai offers steep discounts, deferred payment plans at Novo Land in Tuen Mun, yielding to first-home buyers’ demand for affordable housing
  • US lawmakers urge sanctions against Hong Kong prosecutors for ‘undermining’ city’s rule of law
  • Hong Kong Law Reform Commission proposes 5 new offences to rein in cybercrime, with tougher penalties of up to life imprisonment
  • Thai inmates serving sentence in Hong Kong repatriated, in first such transfer since agreement came into effect in 2000
  • Domestic helper sues Hong Kong boss for sexual harassment, seeks HK$300,000 in damages
  • High-flier schoolgirl aims to add dash of diversity to Hong Kong’s financial world as she earns place at top business school
  • Hong Kong students with special educational needs reveal challenges of studying for university entrance exams
  • Hong Kong to cut out lawyers’ role in mortgage transfers in pilot plan to protect buyers from insolvent legal firms
  • Truck driver arrested after motorcycle passenger dies in Hong Kong crash
  • Hong Kong teen challenging school policy on long hair for boys lodges complaint to equality watchdog, citing ‘gender dysphoria’
  • TikTok owner ByteDance spends record US$2.14 million on US lobbying amid scrutiny over app’s privacy and security practices
  • China to arrest former official of stockpiler Sinograin for corruption
  • China deploys bad-loans expert to clean up Henan’s festering banking and property mess
  • Coronavirus: China’s Shenzhen vows to ‘mobilise all resources’ to curb spread of Covid-19
  • China fines Didi Global US$1.2 billion, ends year-long probe
  • Tesla sells 75 per cent of bitcoin amid cryptocurrency crash, promises ‘record-breaking’ second-half results
  • China seen as better than EU in completing African projects, survey finds
  • China’s top Covid official denies authorities are easing controls and calls for swift response to new outbreaks
  • Joe Biden says call with Xi Jinping due within ‘next 10 days’, casts doubt on Pelosi trip to Taiwan
  • Washington’s consensus on China rarely unites US lawmakers, research finds
  • Distrust and unease loom as South Koreans grow wary of China’s clout
  • Chinese space engineers are developing an exoskeleton suit to give astronauts a boost
  • China lobbying behind scenes at UN to block Xinjiang rights report’s publication, document shows
  • How China’s rare earth dominance spurs US and its allies to diversify supply chain of critical minerals
  • As China, US point blame over global food crisis, what role can their ‘good interactions’ play?
  • Chinese scientists plan ‘disposable’ nuclear reactor for long-range torpedo
  • Trump-era tariffs on Chinese tech imports cost US firms US$32 billion, hurting production and jobs
  • Tech war: ASML’s second-quarter sales data shows shift away from mainland China to Taiwan and South Korea as US lobbying intensifies
  • China prepares to launch space station module for science experiments
  • China rules out ‘excessive stimulus’ to save economy, but Li Keqiang vows to refine coronavirus policy
  • Chinese car chip start-up SiEngine raises US$148 million in new funding as private equity firms invest in semiconductors
  • European Parliament vice-president urges Beijing to open ‘respectful’ dialogue with Taiwan
  • China decries ‘provocations’ after US destroyer sails through Taiwan Strait
  • China’s NetEase recruits Microsoft Xbox talent for new video games studio in Seattle
  • Can a hot-weather fee help protect Asian gig workers languishing in sweltering heat?
  • Coronavirus: Australia sees record deaths as Omicron variants push up hospital cases
  • Singapore’s hanging spate to continue as minister K. Shanmugam vows no let-up in tough laws to fight illegal drug use
  • Australia’s US$22 billion livestock industry under threat as foot and mouth disease found in imported meat
  • Japanese, Irish leaders to keep close eye on China, agree Russia must face ‘severe consequences’
  • Pakistani men accused of gang raping US woman tourist in hotel room
  • Japan sees fewer foreign visitors even after opening border to tourists
  • Can Sri Lanka’s Ranil Wickremesinghe steer the country out of its crisis?
  • Japanese school mocked after punishing student for plucking her eyebrows
  • CIA director estimates 15,000 Russians killed in Ukraine war
  • Die in Ukraine or live in Russia: the poisoned choice for war refugees
  • UN reports numerous killings, rights abuses under Afghanistan’s Taliban
  • Donald Trump bids farewell to ex-wife Ivana at funeral in New York
  • Joe Biden unveils new climate funding, but stops short of declaring emergency
  • Italy PM Mario Draghi’s resignation likely as bid to save government implodes
  • Australia needs better terms with China, yet refuses to meet Beijing halfway
  • Ukraine’s first lady asks US Congress for more weapons against Russia’s ‘Hunger Games’
  • Russia expands goals in Ukraine as war enters fifth month
  • Japan’s drift and Europe’s troubles do not bode well for Pax Americana
  • Britain’s next PM: Liz Truss and Rishi Sunak to slug it out
  • US issues health alert warning new parents about parechovirus – signs, symptoms in babies
  • ‘Hasta la vista, baby,’ says UK’s Boris Johnson as he exits parliament
  • Europe races to cut Russian gas usage amid new Putin warning – we ‘need to be ready’, urges EU chief
  • In or out? Asks Italy’s PM Mario Draghi – awaiting fate in hands of coalition
  • Slowing growth shows China’s old playbook of property and infrastructure investment needs an update
  • Hong Kong stocks fall on concerns about China’s property crisis, while Alibaba paces tech recovery as Didi probe ends
  • Hong Kong’s prime office market takes a breather as banks, other tenants pause after an eight-month leasing spree
  • Chinese headphone maker in Dongguan shuts down, intensifying country’s woes in manufacturing, cross-border e-commerce
  • The real answer to inflation that no one wants to hear: stop spending
  • TikTok’s global expansion stumbles as executives leave and concerns flare over data security
  • Barclays returns to Taiwan with a wholly owned subsidiary after retreat six years ago
  • China’s digital yuan gets fresh push from Beijing, but consumers see little incentive to use e-CNY
  • Mortgage boycott risks manageable for China’s banking system, but small lenders vulnerable, experts say
  • Solving inflation in 2022 means throwing out the 1980s playbook on supply chains
  • Tech giants don’t want us to know how their AI systems work or why they spread racism and untruths. An open-access alternative holds out promise
  • The world’s most powerful passports in 2022: Japan top for fifth year in a row, as Asian countries dominate
  • Jelly cake enthusiast creates incredibly lifelike flower decorations and spreads the word in Hong Kong about the craft, popular in her native Malaysia and Singapore
  • Performance art festival in Hong Kong aims to expose a wider audience to a unique and powerful art form
  • Brad Pitt wore a skirt to premiere of Bullet Train film – it’s not the first time he’s challenged gender norms with fashion
  • In warm-up for an AI Celine Dion, AI choir and dancers accompany human orchestra in Hong Kong concert
  • Video game Stray, set in Hong Kong’s former Kowloon Walled City, in which you play a cat dodging danger, is a cyberpunk delight
  • Is it OK to work out on weekends only? Yes, if daily exercise can’t fit your schedule, researchers show - it’s just as good for you
  • From war to dying industries, abandoned train stations have stories as varied as the structures themselves
  • Hermès Birkin and Hermès Kelly bag collector Jamie Chua, Singapore-based influencer and real-life Crazy Rich Asian, opens up her closet
  • Kim Ga-ram sacked from K-pop’s Le Sserafim over bullying claims – girl group will continue on with five members
  • Why you won’t see a Chinese villain in a Hollywood film any time soon
  • How reading The Wisdom of the Enneagram changed the life of a former businesswoman and social enterprise founder
  • Who’s the richest Wooga Squad member? Net worths, ranked: BTS’ V, Park Seo-joon, Park Hyung-sik, Choi Woo-shik and Peakboy are set to star in Disney+’s In the Soop, but who has the most millions?
  • Love it or hate it: why is 2000s fashion so controversial in K-pop? Blackpink’s Jennie and Girls’ Generation’s Yoona show off the style on Instagram, but not all millennials and Gen Zs are on board
  • Manolo Blahnik wins decades-long legal battle in China after trademark dispute: the shoemaker was made famous by Sex and the City, and joins Disney and Nike’s NBA star Michael Jordan in copyright victories
  • 5 times K-pop idols were swindled or betrayed by agency staff: BTS and Seventeen had their personal info leaked, Blackpink’s Lisa had money stolen, and Momoland’s Nancy was filmed undressing
  • Who is James Middleton, Kate Middleton’s younger brother? He lives a separate life from the British royal family, but is a ‘cool’ uncle to William and Kate’s son Prince George
  • Who will be the next James Bond? 10 men – and 1 woman – in the running after Daniel Craig, from Robert Pattinson, Harry Styles and Henry Cavill, to Tom Hardy, Idris Elba and even Lashana Lynch
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Tokyo Olympic aftermath still being untangled a year later

  • Ukrainian refugees forced to escape to enemy soil in Russia
  • Indonesian court rejects call to legalize medicinal marijuana
  • Promoting women will boost economic potential of U.S., S.Korea--Yellen
  • Three long-lost paintings by Taro Okamoto found in Paris
  • TASTE OF LIFE/ Rice bowl dish with egg, Chinese chives and tomato: Life-changing dish served by a neighbor led to a career in cooking

  • EDITORIAL: Japan and South Korea off to a good start in mending ties
  • EDITORIAL: State funeral for Abe mired in controversy like political legacy
  • VOX POPULI: State funeral for Abe will only serve to extol his actions in life
  • VOX POPULI: Entering summer of discontent as 7th wave of pandemic strikes
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.content:

entry.title: Taipei Times: 21 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
